name: Health
description: A core vitality and failure state that resolves damage, healing, downed/death transitions, and recovery from combat, hazards, disease, starvation, dehydration, and exposure.
design_notes: Survival health is the final consequence layer for many systems, so damage channels, recovery sources, and death transitions must be traceable. Designers should decide which damage_resolver owns health transactions, how hunger, thirst, temperature, poison, injuries, armor, invulnerability, and healing feed health, and which UI message explains why vitality changed.
design_purpose: Represent survivability and make damage or neglect carry meaningful stakes.
Connect combat, environment, status effects, medicine, rest, and food systems.
Provide a recoverable failure buffer before death, downed state, or respawn.
player_fantasy: The player feels fragile enough to respect danger but capable of recovering through preparation.

parameters.name: health_stat_id
parameters.description: Vitality stat identifier used by damage, recovery, UI, saves, and death logic.

parameters.name: max_health_points
parameters.description: Maximum vitality before equipment, difficulty, injury, or progression modifiers.

parameters.name: damage_channel_id
parameters.typical_range: bleed, starvation, cold, melee, poison, fall
parameters.description: Source channel used for resistance, invulnerability checks, logging, UI explanation, and effect routing.

parameters.name: recovery_source_id
parameters.description: Item, rest state, or ability that restores health through a validated recovery transaction.

parameters.name: downed_state_threshold_id
parameters.description: Rule that decides whether depletion commits death, enters downed state, or opens a revival window.

parameters.name: anti_burst_buffer_ms
parameters.description: Short grace window used to prevent multiple survival damage ticks from instantly stacking.

edge_cases: Starvation, dehydration, cold, poison, and combat damage resolve on the same tick and must produce one ordered health transaction.
A healing item is used while the player is entering a downed, revive, or death transition.
Temporary max-health reduction changes current health, death threshold, and UI warnings after save/load.
Invulnerability frames, armor, or resistance modifiers change after a damage event is queued but before it resolves.
Checkpoint or respawn restoration must clear bleedout, pending damage ticks, duplicate death rewards, and low-health UI state.
Client prediction shows recovery but server authority rejects the item, damage transaction, or downed-state transition.
common_bugs: Damage over time stacks every frame because survival ticks are not rate-limited by damage_channel_id or damage_transaction_id.
Healing consumes items but fails when max health modifiers update during the same transaction.
Death logic fires twice and creates duplicate drops, ragdolls, revive prompts, or respawn checkpoints.
Health is clamped after UI update, so the bar briefly shows negative health or overheal that gameplay already rejected.
Save/load restores current health but not pending bleedout, poison, revive, or invulnerability state.
UI health bars hide the source of survival damage, making hunger, thirst, or temperature feel like random health loss.
balancing_notes: Health should give players enough buffer to diagnose survival problems before dying.
Passive regeneration can reduce frustration but may weaken hunger, thirst, and medicine loops.
Multiple survival penalties should compound carefully; simultaneous systems can create sudden failure spirals.
accessibility_notes: Show damage source text or icons when health is lost from survival systems, not only combat.
Offer reduced screen blood, pulse effects, or low-health audio intensity while preserving readable danger cues.

implementation_notes.unity: Route all health changes through a damage and recovery service keyed by damage_channel_id or recovery_source_id. Apply anti_burst_buffer_ms before stacking survival ticks, and store health, modifiers, downed state, and pending bleedout in save data.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep health in a survival stat node or autoload service that receives typed damage events. Healing items should request a recovery transaction that consumes inventory, clamps max health modifiers, and emits one UI update.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use a replicated attribute set or component for health and survival damage channels. The server should own death, downed, and recovery transactions, while clients display predicted healing only until authority confirms.
implementation_notes.web: Model health changes as events in the simulation layer with source ids and tick ids. This makes local saves, replay, and browser reloads less likely to duplicate damage or recovery effects.

relationships.reason: Health damage transactions can produce hit-stun or recovery events after damage resolution.
Health depletion can enter a downed state that enables teammate revive interactions.

example_games.title: Resident Evil 4
example_games.note: Uses health recovery items and danger states as part of survival pressure.

example_games.title: Doom
example_games.note: Uses health pickups and damage state as visible combat survival pressure.

example_games.title: The Long Dark
example_games.note: Connects health loss to exposure, starvation, dehydration, and injuries.

example_games.title: Hollow Knight
example_games.note: Uses health masks and recovery windows as a visible survival resource.

example_games.title: Minecraft
example_games.note: Uses health hearts, damage, and recovery as visible survival state.

example_games.title: Dark Souls
example_games.note: Uses health state, healing windows, and death recovery pressure in combat.

example_games.title: Subnautica
example_games.note: Uses health loss and recovery alongside oxygen, food, water, and hazards.

example_games.title: Left 4 Dead
example_games.note: Uses health, incapacitation, and recovery pressure during co-op survival encounters.

example_games.title: Half-Life 2
example_games.note: Uses health pickups and damage state as visible first-person survival pressure.

example_games.title: The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time
example_games.note: Uses heart containers and health recovery as visible adventure survival state.

example_games.title: Spelunky
example_games.note: Uses limited health to make traps, falls, and enemy hits persistent run pressure.

example_games.title: Alien: Isolation
example_games.note: Uses health loss and recovery items as visible survival pressure.

example_games.title: Super Metroid
example_games.note: Uses energy tanks and health pickups as visible survival state.

example_games.title: Terraria
example_games.note: Uses health hearts and healing items during exploration and combat.
